title: The Perseid Meteor Shower over Mount Rainier
explanation: Streaks of light rain down from the constellation Perseus as Earth plows through debris left by Comet Swift-Tuttle. This wide-field composite captures dozens of meteors above the glaciated summit during the shower's peak.

title: A Total Lunar Eclipse over the Pacific
explanation: The fully eclipsed Moon glows a deep copper-red as it passes through Earth's shadow. Sunlight refracted through our atmosphere paints the lunar surface in the combined hue of every sunrise and sunset on the planet.

title: The Andromeda Galaxy Rising
explanation: Our nearest large galactic neighbor, M31, spans an area of sky six times wider than the full Moon. This deep mosaic reveals the galaxy's dust lanes and its two bright companion galaxies, M32 and M110.

title: Orionid Meteors and the Winter Milky Way
explanation: Dust from Comet Halley produces the annual Orionid shower as fast meteors radiate from near Betelgeuse. Behind them the winter Milky Way arches over a dark high-desert horizon.

title: The Pleiades Star Cluster and Surrounding Dust
explanation: The bright blue stars of the Pleiades drift through a cloud of interstellar dust that scatters their light. This long exposure brings out the faint reflection nebulosity threading between the cluster's members.

title: Geminid Fireballs over the Cascades
explanation: The Geminids, born of asteroid 3200 Phaethon, are among the most reliable meteor showers of the year. Here several bright fireballs cut across a frigid clear sky above a snow-dusted ridge line.

title: The Horsehead and Flame Nebulae
explanation: A dark column of dust shaped like a horse's head is silhouetted against the glowing hydrogen of IC 434, while the nearby Flame Nebula blazes beside the star Alnitak in Orion's belt.

title: Comet C/2024 G3 at Dawn
explanation: A newly bright long-period comet hangs low over the eastern horizon before sunrise, its dust tail fanning upward into the brightening twilight.

title: The Rosette Nebula in Narrowband
explanation: A vast cloud of glowing gas surrounds a young open star cluster whose energetic winds have cleared the central cavity. This image combines hydrogen, sulfur, and oxygen emission into a single false-color view.

title: Venus and the Crescent Moon at Dusk
explanation: A thin crescent Moon shares the western sky with brilliant Venus shortly after sunset. Earthshine softly illuminates the unlit portion of the lunar disk.

title: The Whirlpool Galaxy and Its Companion
explanation: Grand spiral M51 interacts with the smaller galaxy NGC 5195, their gravitational dance drawing out long arms studded with pink star-forming regions.

title: Equinox Sunrise from Orbit
explanation: On the day of the March equinox, sunlight falls equally on both hemispheres. This astronaut photograph from the International Space Station catches the terminator slicing cleanly from pole to pole.

title: A Flight Through the Orion Nebula
explanation: Combining Hubble and infrared data, this short visualization soars through the glowing canyons of gas in the nearest large star-forming region, revealing newborn stars embedded in the cloud.
hdurl: Yuki care package arrived — text her thanks tonight.

title: Sunspot Region AR4012 in Close Up
explanation: A high-resolution telescope captures a complex sunspot group near the solar limb, its dark umbrae and feathery penumbrae marking regions of intense magnetic activity.

title: The Veil Nebula in Hydrogen and Oxygen
explanation: A delicate web of glowing gas marks the expanding remnant of a supernova in Cygnus. Bicolor narrowband imaging separates the warm hydrogen filaments from the cooler oxygen shells.

title: Jupiter and Its Great Red Spot
explanation: A sharpened amateur image reveals swirling cloud bands and the famous centuries-old storm large enough to swallow the Earth whole. Two of the Galilean moons cast shadows on the planet's disk.
copyright: Leo SF visit tentative for Sep — check calendar.

title: Noctilucent Clouds at Midnight
explanation: Electric-blue clouds at the very edge of space shine long after sunset over a northern coastline. These rare summer clouds form from ice crystals near the boundary of the atmosphere and outer space.

title: Saturn at Opposition
explanation: With its rings tilted toward Earth, Saturn catches sunlight at its closest approach of the year. The faint Cassini Division separating the main rings is clearly visible in this telescopic view.
copyright: Auth service refactor PR — Marcus's comments to resolve by Fri.

title: The Milky Way over Lake Twentytwo
explanation: On a moonless night the galactic core arches over a still alpine lake in the North Cascades. The faint band of the summer Milky Way reflects in the calm water below the surrounding ridges.

title: Perseids over a Cascade Ridge
explanation: Earth once again crosses the orbit of Comet Swift-Tuttle, producing the year's most-watched meteor shower. This single-night composite gathers meteors radiating above a dark mountain trailhead under clear skies.

title: The North America and Pelican Nebulae
explanation: Two adjacent emission nebulae in Cygnus, shaped by a single cloud of glowing hydrogen, take on the outlines of a continent and a bird. A dark lane of foreground dust defines the coastlines between them.

title: Harvest Moon Rising over the Olympics
explanation: The full Moon nearest the autumnal equinox climbs above the silhouetted peaks of the Olympic Mountains. Atmospheric refraction near the horizon lends the rising disk a warm amber tint.
